title: Devices Affected by Active Advisories

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracks Cisco PSIRT security advisories affecting managed devices, providing evidence of vulnerability awareness and remediation tracking as required by NIST 800-53 Flaw Remediation.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates continuous monitoring of vendor-published security advisories for all managed network infrastructure.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search over index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory scheduled daily; advisory counts and affected device lists exported to compliance dashboard.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Contributes to vulnerability scanning by tracking known vendor-published vulnerabilities, though it does not replace active scanning tools.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides a vendor-authoritative vulnerability feed for network infrastructure to complement active vulnerability scanning.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard panel showing advisory severity distribution and affected device counts, refreshed with each TA poll cycle.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory`. from the `securityadvisory` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/security-advisory/advisory`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per advisory-device pair. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `advisoryId` (string), `advisoryTitle` (string), `severity` (string), `cveId` (string), `deviceCount` (integer), `deviceId` (string), `fixedVersions` (array of strings), `publicationDate` (string). CIM data model: Vulnerabilities.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" | stats count as advisory_count values(advisoryId) as advisories values(severity) as severities by deviceId, deviceName, platformId | eval has_critical=if(searchmatch("*CRITICAL*"),1,0) | sort -has_critical -advisory_count
description: Maps security advisories to specific devices, showing which devices have the most advisories and which have critical-severity vulnerabilities.
value: Knowing which devices are most exposed enables risk-based patching prioritization — fix the devices with the most critical advisories first.
implementation: Enable the `securityadvisory` input. If `searchmatch` is noisy on multivalue `severities`, consider `mvfind` or `where mvfilter` patterns instead after validating the field format.
visualization: Table (advisory_count, severities, advisories by device), bar chart of top at-risk deviceId.
knownFalsePositives:
**Same device appearing in multiple advisories inflating the affected-device count.** A device running vulnerable software may be affected by multiple PSIRTs simultaneously, appearing multiple times in the per-device advisory view. Distinguish by using `dc(advisoryId)` per device to understand the distinct advisory count. No suppression needed — each advisory is a separate finding.

**Lab or non-production device affected by critical advisories.** Lab devices running intentionally outdated firmware may be affected by many advisories that do not warrant operational urgency. Distinguish by checking whether the device is in a non-production `siteId`. Suppress by filtering lab devices from the operational advisory dashboard using a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup.

**Device UUID change after RMA making the same physical device appear as a new affected device.** After RMA replacement, the new device has a different `deviceId` but the same `hostname`. The advisory may show both the old and new UUIDs as affected. Distinguish by checking whether `hostname` matches between old and new `deviceId` values. Suppress by correlating with inventory data to deduplicate by `hostname`.

**Advisory scan incomplete for large inventories.** In environments with thousands of devices, the PSIRT scan may not complete within a single poll cycle, showing an incomplete list of affected devices. Distinguish by checking whether `dc(deviceId)` increases between consecutive polls for the same `advisoryId`. Suppress by waiting for the count to stabilize (2-3 poll cycles) before using the affected-device list for operational decisions.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **securityadvisory** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory`.
• At least one active advisory with `deviceCount > 0` for meaningful results.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls the security advisory API, which includes per-advisory device counts. The `deviceCount` field indicates how many managed devices are affected by each advisory.

Key fields:
• `advisoryId`: advisory identifier.
• `deviceCount`: count of affected devices.
• `severity`: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFORMATIONAL.
• `cveId`: CVE identifier(s).
• `fixedVersions`: software versions that fix the vulnerability (if available).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" deviceCount>0
| stats latest(deviceCount) as affected_devices latest(severity) as severity values(cveId) as cves values(fixedVersions) as fixes by advisoryId
| eval has_fix=if(isnotnull(fixes) AND fixes!="", "Fix Available", "No Fix")
| eval risk_score=case(severity="CRITICAL", affected_devices*10, severity="HIGH", affected_devices*5, severity="MEDIUM", affected_devices*2, 1==1, affected_devices)
| sort -risk_score
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`deviceCount>0`**: Filters to advisories that actually affect managed devices — excludes informational advisories with zero impact.
• **`has_fix`**: Identifies whether a fixed software version is available, which determines whether remediation is possible through firmware upgrade.
• **`risk_score`**: Weighted risk score combining severity and affected device count. CRITICAL advisories are weighted 10x, HIGH 5x, etc.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Security Advisories. Select a specific advisory and compare the affected device count with the Splunk results.
• **Risk score validation:** Verify the top-risk advisory in Splunk matches the highest-severity advisory with the most affected devices in the Catalyst Center UI.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Risk-ranked advisory table, affected device count by severity (stacked bar), and a drill-down to the specific devices affected by each advisory.
• **Alert:** Trigger when a CRITICAL advisory affects >10 devices (`severity="CRITICAL" AND affected_devices>10`). Route to the security and network teams.
• **Remediation prioritization:** Use the `has_fix` field to focus upgrade efforts on advisories where remediation is possible.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **`deviceCount` always showing 0:** The PSIRT scan may not have completed. Wait 24 hours after Catalyst Center discovers new advisories for the device count to stabilize.
• **Same device counted in multiple advisories:** This is expected — a device may be affected by multiple vulnerabilities simultaneously. Use `dc(advisoryId) by deviceId` to understand per-device exposure.
• **`fixedVersions` field empty:** Cisco may not yet have released a fix. Track these advisories separately for risk acceptance.
• If data is not arriving for `cisco:dnac:securityadvisory`, check that the `securityadvisory` input is enabled in the TA configuration and that the Catalyst Center API credentials have not expired.
grandmaExplanation: We show you exactly which devices are affected by security warnings and which ones have the most serious vulnerabilities to fix first.
